product.name: Barbell Easy Calculator
product.tagline: Symmetric barbell loading without gym-floor math.

hero.headline: Load the bar in seconds, not in your head.
hero.subhead: Barbell Easy Calculator is a cross-platform lifting utility focused on fast, symmetric loading with exact and nearest results.

summary: This project packages two practical workflows: enter a target total and get a clear loading plan, or build the bar visually by adding and removing mirrored plate pairs. It keeps the experience offline-first and geared toward real gym constraints such as plate availability.
audience: Lifters who need fast and clear plate loading guidance
Home gym users managing limited plate inventory
coreProblems: Mental math slows setup between sets
Exact targets are not always possible with available plates
Many calculators ignore real inventory limits
Visual loading and numerical loading are often disconnected

features.title: Deterministic symmetric solver
features.summary: Uses fixed strategy rules to return repeatable, mirrored loading outcomes.

features.title: Two loading modes
features.summary: Supports target-driven calculation and interactive visual build flows.

features.title: Inventory-aware gym profiles
features.summary: Can limit recommendations based on what a gym profile says is actually available.

features.title: Multiple strategy styles
features.summary: Includes Exact Match Priority, Fewest Plates, Easiest to Load, and Nearest Achievable.

features.title: Offline local persistence
features.summary: Stores settings, profiles, and saved loads locally using Capacitor Preferences.

workflows.title: Target Weight Mode
workflows.summary: Set unit, bar, and target total, then review exact or nearest per-side instructions.

workflows.title: Visual Build Mode
workflows.summary: Tap plate sizes to add mirrored pairs and tap loaded plates to remove pairs live.

workflows.title: Gym Profile Setup
workflows.summary: Adjust bars, enabled plates, and inventory assumptions for different training environments.

proofPoints.label: Core modes
proofPoints.note: Target Weight and Visual Build workflows are documented in the repository.

proofPoints.label: Solver strategies
proofPoints.note: Strategy set is defined in models and covered by tests.

proofPoints.label: Units
proofPoints.note: Both systems are represented in presets and tests.

proofPoints.label: Current checks
proofPoints.note: Local run on 2026-03-17: vitest suite passed and production build succeeded.

proofPoints.label: Native surface
proofPoints.note: Repository includes Capacitor config and iOS project scaffolding.

faq.question: Is this already distributed in app stores?
faq.answer: Store distribution is not stated in this repository. The repo includes iOS/Android project scaffolding and native open/sync commands.

faq.question: Does it work without a backend?
faq.answer: The MVP scope is local-first with Capacitor Preferences and no backend service described.

faq.question: Can it handle impossible exact targets?
faq.answer: Yes. The solver supports nearest lower and higher outcomes when exact loading is unavailable.

faq.question: Can I model my own gym setup?
faq.answer: Yes. Gym profiles in the repo include bars, plate sets, and inventory assumptions, and the UI supports customization.
